From: marie.lefebvre@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: project-team-ngis@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: NGIS Phase 3 — Weekly Status / Mise à jour hebdomadaire
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 10:05:00 -0500

Hello team / Bonjour l'équipe,

ENGLISH SECTION:

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: A 2-week delay in the delivery of critical network equipment (Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches) directly overlaps with the scheduled migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The project lacks equipment for the mandatory staging and testing phase, which was originally scheduled to begin on March 6.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Supply chain vulnerability due to single-sourcing through a specific regional warehouse (Montreal), leaving no immediate redundancy for equipment fulfillment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: Potential for further schedule slippage if alternative routing is attempted, as the backup depot (Vancouver) adds significant transit time.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Risk of missing the Q2 migration window entirely, which may have cascading effects on other government IT dependencies or fiscal year deadlines.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Implicit risk of increased project costs (budget overrun) due to 'around the clock' expedited shipping efforts or potential overtime for SSC staff to compress the staging timeline once equipment arrives.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: Systemic failure risk at the supplier level; the warehouse management system failure may indicate broader operational instability at TechSupplier Solutions Inc.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments

risks.description: The project has already consumed three weeks of schedule contingency due to infrastructure assessment delays, reducing the buffer available for future unforeseen issues before the Q3 2026 deadline.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment phase required a slight adjustment of approximately three weeks... this has been fully accommodated within the project's existing schedule contingency.

risks.description: The project is currently trending 14% over the approved budget envelope due to newly discovered middleware remediation requirements, requiring formal approval for additional funds.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing approximately 14% above the approved envelope of $2,400,000.

risks.description: Discovery of middleware remediation requirements suggests that the original scope baseline was incomplete, posing a risk of further technical 'unknown unknowns' during the migration phase.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment identified additional middleware remediation requirements not captured in the original scope baseline.

risks.description: The project is entering a procurement phase in Q2 2026; any delays in government procurement processes or lack of qualified vendors could delay the implementation start date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team looks forward to entering the implementation phase in Q2 2026 following completion of the procurement activities.

risks.description: Potential misalignment between the project's 'Green' status and the actual financial/scope health, which may lead to stakeholder friction when the 14% budget overrun is formally presented.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Status: GREEN... The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing approximately 14% above the approved envelope

risks.description: The 'refinement of the cost model' and 'updated cost projections' suggest that the final migration costs are still volatile and may exceed the current $340,000 overrun once implementation begins.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The findings from this assessment are being used to refine our cost model... We are in the process of finalizing updated cost projections

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: The delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches is delayed by 14 days, missing the required on-site date for staging and testing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The delay in equipment delivery directly conflicts with the Q2 migration window, potentially forcing a postponement of the entire Phase 3 migration.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The project is exposed to a single point of failure due to single-sourcing through a specific warehouse location.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: The warehouse management system (WMS) failure at the supplier may indicate broader systemic issues or potential data/order integrity risks.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments

risks.description: The 2-week delay eliminates the buffer for 'staging and testing,' which may lead to a rushed technical implementation and increased risk of network failure during migration.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Alternative logistics routes (Vancouver) are inefficient and would introduce further delays, limiting the supplier's ability to recover the schedule.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Potential for increased project costs (budget overrun) due to 'around the clock' expedited shipping efforts or idle technical staff waiting for equipment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches, pushing arrival from March 6 to March 20, 2026 — after the March 8 migration window opens, making it impossible to complete staging and testing before the window begins.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a critical supply chain vulnerability with no readily available equivalent alternative routing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Alternative routing via Vancouver depot would extend the delay by an additional 3–5 business days beyond March 20, potentially pushing delivery to approximately March 25–27, further compressing or eliminating any remaining schedule buffer.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre may not be fully resolved, creating risk that the March 20 revised delivery date is itself optimistic and subject to further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Insufficient staging and testing time: even if equipment arrives March 20, there is no time allocated for pre-migration staging and testing that was explicitly required to be completed before the March 8 migration window, risking untested equipment being deployed into production.
affected_stakeholders: SSC end users dependent on migrated services

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Q2 migration timeline for NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration is at risk of being missed entirely, which may trigger contractual penalties, government reporting obligations, or downstream program dependencies tied to Q2 delivery commitments.
affected_stakeholders: Treasury Board or other oversight bodies

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Potential budget impact from expedited shipping costs, emergency procurement of alternative equipment, or costs associated with rescheduling migration activities (e.g., contractor overtime, extended facility bookings).

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: Resource scheduling risk: technical staff, contractors, and data centre personnel mobilized for the March 8 migration window may need to be stood down and rescheduled, risking resource unavailability for a rescheduled window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Compliance risk: as a Government of Canada (SSC) project, NGIS Phase 3 may be subject to Treasury Board project management policy, PSPC supply arrangements, or contractual SLAs that impose obligations when delivery milestones are missed, and the delay may trigger mandatory reporting or change control processes.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Implicit communication and governance risk: the delay was first discussed verbally on a Friday call and only confirmed in writing the following Monday, suggesting a gap in formal change notification procedures that could delay escalation and decision-making.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration.

risks.description: Hidden dependency risk: the email reveals no contingency or fallback plan exists for equipment non-delivery, suggesting the project has no documented risk mitigation strategy for supply chain disruption on critical path hardware.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Please let me know how we can best support your planning activities during this disruption.

risks.description: Vendor reliability risk: TechSupplier's WMS failure and single-source supply model expose SSC to ongoing supplier concentration risk for future phases or procurements, with no indication of a secondary qualified vendor.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse.

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: A 2-week delay in the delivery of critical network equipment (Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches) directly overlaps with the scheduled migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The project lacks equipment for the mandatory staging and testing phase, which was originally scheduled to begin on March 6.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The supply chain for critical infrastructure is single-sourced through a single warehouse, creating a single point of failure (SPOF) for equipment procurement.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: The delay in equipment delivery threatens the viability of the entire Q2 migration timeline, likely causing a domino effect on subsequent project phases.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: The vendor's internal IT failure (Warehouse Management System) indicates a dependency on the vendor's operational stability which has already proven unreliable.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments

risks.description: Geographic constraints and logistics limitations prevent rapid mitigation; alternative shipping routes are slower than the delayed primary route.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Potential for increased costs (budget overrun) due to 'around the clock' expedited shipping and potential overtime for SSC staff to compress the staging/testing window once equipment arrives.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches, pushing arrival from March 6 to March 20 — after the March 8 migration window opens, making the window unusable without equipment on site.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a single point of failure with no rapid alternative; any further disruption at that warehouse could extend delays indefinitely.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The only identified alternative routing (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days on top of the already-delayed March 20 date, potentially pushing delivery to March 25–27 and further compressing or eliminating the Q2 migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre. The failure is ongoing and its resolution timeline is unconfirmed, meaning the March 20 revised date is itself an estimate subject to further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Staging and testing of the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches requires equipment on site by March 6. With delivery now on March 20 at earliest, there is zero staging and testing time before the migration window, risking undetected hardware or configuration defects entering production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The delay places significant pressure on the Q2 migration timeline. If the migration window cannot be rescheduled within Q2, the project may miss its fiscal-year or contractual delivery commitments, with potential budget and contractual consequences.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Delay in equipment delivery may trigger contract penalty clauses, SLA breaches, or require formal contract amendments with TechSupplier, creating compliance and procurement governance obligations under Government of Canada contracting rules.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6.

risks.description: Migration window rescheduling may require re-booking of technical resources (engineers, contractors, data centre staff) who may not be available on alternative dates, creating a resource availability risk and potential cost increases for rebooking.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline. To be transparent: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse.

risks.description: Rescheduling the migration window or expediting alternative logistics (e.g., Vancouver depot routing, premium freight) will likely incur unplanned costs not budgeted in the current project plan.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: The delay was communicated informally via email following a phone call, with no documented escalation path or formal change-control record. This creates a governance and audit risk if the project is subject to formal change management requirements under SSC or Treasury Board policies.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration.

risks.description: Downstream project activities (network configuration, server rack installation, application cutover, user acceptance testing) that depend on the switches being in place are implicitly blocked, creating a cascade of schedule delays across the broader NGIS Phase 3 programme.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The WMS failure at TechSupplier may be affecting other equipment orders beyond the Catalyst 9500 switches (e.g., other hardware for NGIS Phase 3), but this is not confirmed in the communication, representing a hidden scope-of-impact risk.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

metadata.subject: URGENT: ITSG-33 Compliance Gap — NGIS Phase 3 Network Migration

risks.description: The NGIS Phase 3 Network Migration scheduled for March 18 is at risk of delay due to unresolved security compliance findings.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Until this finding is resolved, the March 18 migration window should be considered at risk.

risks.description: Failure to resolve the IA-5 control finding may lead to the suspension of SSC's Protected B, Medium Integrity, Medium Availability (PBMM) cloud hosting authorization.
affected_stakeholders: All Government of Canada departments using shared AD

evidence_refs.excerpt: SSC's PBMM cloud hosting authorization may be placed on hold, potentially affecting other GC-Cloud workloads depending on the shared AD infrastructure.

risks.description: The project lacks required technical documentation for Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) architecture, indicating a gap in the Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) package.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Specifically, the Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) package is missing the Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) architecture documentation required under ITSG-33 Annex 3A, Control IA-5(1).

risks.description: The project team is under extreme time pressure to produce complex technical documentation and coordinate high-level meetings within a 5-7 day window, risking poor quality or burnout.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation addressing Control IA-5 to this office no later than March 12, 2026.

risks.description: Implicit risk of cost overruns due to emergency remediation efforts, potential migration rescheduling fees, and extended project timelines.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation... Until this finding is resolved, the March 18 migration window should be considered at risk.

risks.description: Hidden risk of systemic security vulnerabilities in the target domain controllers if the migration proceeds with undocumented or insufficient privileged account management.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The proposed Active Directory migration plan does not include documentation for privileged account management controls on the target domain controllers.

evidence_refs.excerpt: potentially affecting other GC-Cloud workloads depending on the shared AD infrastructure.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation addressing Control IA-5 to this office no later than March 12, 2026.

evidence_refs.excerpt: A meeting between the SSC CISO office and the NGIS project team must be scheduled for the week of March 9 to review the PAW architecture.

risks.description: Implicit gap in project governance and quality assurance: The absence of PAW architecture documentation in the SA&A package suggests the project's internal security review process failed to catch this gap before the external TBS audit, indicating a systemic weakness in pre-audit compliance verification.

metadata.subject: Site A AD Migration — Progress Update

risks.description: Critical hardware dependency: The migration relies on the delivery and installation of Catalyst 9500 network switches. Any delay in shipping or installation will halt final connectivity testing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The only dependency is the network switch delivery — we need the Catalyst 9500s in place before we can do final network connectivity testing for the DCs.

risks.description: Tight schedule buffer: There is only a four-day buffer between the planned completion of staging (March 14) and the hard migration window (March 18). Any technical issues during staging will likely push the Go-Live date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: targeting completion by March 14 to give us four days of buffer before the March 18 migration window.

risks.description: Legacy GPO Migration Complexity: Consolidating 47 legacy GPOs from different sites (B and C) into Site A carries a risk of unforeseen policy conflicts or 'ghost' settings that may not have been captured in the manual review.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I've reviewed the 47 legacy GPOs from Sites B and C. 23 are duplicates that can be deleted. 24 unique GPOs will be migrated.

risks.description: Patching Gap: Servers were patched as of March 1. If the migration occurs on March 18, there is a risk of 'Patch Tuesday' (typically the second Tuesday of the month, March 10) introducing new critical vulnerabilities or required updates that haven't been applied or tested.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Windows Server patching is complete (all critical patches as of March 1 applied).

risks.description: Single Point of Failure in Review: The migration script and security compliance review were performed by a single individual (Dave Chen). This represents a resource/knowledge silo risk if the script fails during production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The migration script is ready and has been reviewed by Dave Chen for security policy compliance.

risks.description: Rollback Downtime: While the rollback procedure is tested, a 45-minute rollback time plus the time taken to identify a failure could exceed the maintenance window, leading to service outages for Site A.

evidence_refs.excerpt: estimated rollback time is 45 minutes from Go-Live.

risks.description: Environment Disparity: Schema extensions were successful in 'test,' but production environments often have latent metadata or lingering objects not present in test, which can cause schema failures during the promotion to staging/production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The AD schema extension ran successfully in the test environment last night... Ready to promote to staging this weekend.

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: A 2-week delay in the delivery of critical network equipment (Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches) directly overlaps with the scheduled migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The project lacks equipment for the mandatory staging and testing phase, which was originally scheduled to begin on March 6.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The supply chain for critical infrastructure is single-sourced through a single warehouse, creating a single point of failure (SPOF) for equipment procurement.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: The delay in equipment delivery threatens the viability of the Q2 migration timeline, potentially causing a domino effect on subsequent project phases.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: The vendor's internal system failure (Warehouse Management System) indicates a dependency on the vendor's operational stability which is currently compromised.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments

risks.description: Geographic/Logistical risk: Alternative shipping routes (Vancouver) are inefficient and would introduce further delays, limiting recovery options.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Implicit Budget Risk: Expediting shipments and working 'around the clock' may lead to unbudgeted shipping premiums or labor costs to catch up on the lost 2-week window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: A 2-week delay in the delivery of critical network equipment (Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches) directly overlaps with the scheduled migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: The project lacks equipment for the mandatory staging and testing phase, which was originally scheduled to begin on March 6.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The delay threatens the viability of the Q2 migration timeline, potentially causing a domino effect on subsequent project phases.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Supply chain vulnerability due to single-sourcing; the project is reliant on a single warehouse in Montreal with no immediate backup.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: Logistical inefficiency; alternative shipping routes (Vancouver) are slower than the current delayed timeline, providing no viable contingency for faster recovery.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Potential for increased project costs (budget overrun) due to 'around the clock' expedited labor and potential penalties or overtime for SSC staff to compress the migration window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: Systemic failure risk; the supplier's Warehouse Management System (WMS) failure may indicate broader operational instability or risk of further data/shipment errors.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments

risks.description: Missed migration window; if the March 8 window is missed, there may be compliance or service level agreement (SLA) impacts regarding the data centre migration deadline.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches, pushing arrival from March 6 to March 20, which is 12 days after the migration window opens on March 8. Staging and testing cannot begin on schedule.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a critical supply chain vulnerability. Any further disruption at that single node has no fast recovery path.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse

risks.description: Alternative routing via Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days beyond the already-delayed March 20 date, meaning the fallback option does not resolve the schedule gap and could push delivery to approximately March 25–27.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre. The failure began February 26 and its resolution timeline is unconfirmed, meaning the March 20 revised date itself carries uncertainty and could slip further.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26

risks.description: Q2 migration timeline for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration is at risk. If the migration window cannot be met, downstream Q2 commitments, contractual milestones, or government fiscal-year deliverables may be missed.
affected_stakeholders: Government of Canada stakeholders dependent on NGIS

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Insufficient staging and testing time: Alice's email specifies equipment must arrive by March 6 for staging and testing before the March 8 migration window. With delivery now on March 20, there is zero staging buffer, risking untested equipment being deployed or the migration window being missed entirely.
affected_stakeholders: End users dependent on migrated services

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Budget risk arising from potential need to expedite shipping, engage alternative suppliers at premium cost, extend contractor or staff time due to schedule slippage, or pay penalties for missed contractual milestones.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: Resource scheduling risk: technical staff, contractors, and data centre personnel likely booked around the March 8 migration window. A delay to March 20 or later may result in resource conflicts, unavailability, or additional costs to re-schedule specialized personnel.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Compliance and contractual risk: SSC is a Government of Canada entity subject to Treasury Board and PSPC procurement rules. A supplier's inability to deliver on contracted dates may trigger contract performance clauses, reporting obligations, or require a formal contract amendment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6.

risks.description: Communication and governance risk: the delay was first discussed verbally on a Friday call and only confirmed in writing the following Monday morning. This informal, reactive communication pattern may indicate inadequate supplier performance monitoring and early-warning processes, increasing the likelihood of future surprises.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

parsed_json.description: Compliance and contractual risk: SSC is a Government of Canada entity subject to Treasury Board and PSPC procurement rules. parsed_json.description: Communication and governance risk: the delay was first discussed verbally on a Friday call and only confirmed in writing the following Monday morning. parsed_json.description: Reputational and service continuity risk to SSC: NGIS (Next Generation Infrastructure Services) likely underpins services delivered to other Government of Canada departments. A delayed migration could affect service levels promised to client departments.
parsed_json.evidence_excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

  "reasoning": "The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clear scope expansion. While the rationale offered — co-location of contractors, EOL hardware, efficiency gains — is operationally reasonable, it does not constitute an approved scope item. out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4

Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative

  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A (consolidation to a single Site A target only — no secondary or DR site identified); DELIVERABLES: D-006 Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — April 30, 2026 (scope is bounded to Site A as the sole destination); CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget $2,400,000 CAD — budget variances exceeding 15% require Treasury Board submission; All scope changes must follow SSC Project Change Management Process with written Project Sponsor approval.",
  "suggested_action": "Project Manager Alice Tran should proceed with the committed preliminary DR site options analysis (due March 15, 2026) and formally document this request as a candidate Change Request under the SSC Project Change Management Process. The options analysis must include: (1) a full scope impact assessment against SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001, (2) a cost estimate to determine whether the addition would breach the 15% budget variance threshold triggering mandatory Treasury Board submission, (3) a schedule impact assessment against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint and June 30, 2026 closure deadline — both of which are at significant risk given the March 8, 2026 date of this request, (4) identification of any SA&A implications under ITSG-33 for a new colocation facility, and (5) a recommendation on whether the DR site is better deferred to a subsequent phase (e.g., NGIS Phase 4 or a standalone DR initiative). No procurement, design, or implementation activity related to the DR site may commence until a formal Change Request receives written approval from the Project Sponsor and, if cost thresholds are breached, Treasury Board notification is completed.",

ADM Clarke explicitly acknowledged the request falls outside the current SOW. The addition of a new physical site (Borden or Miramichi), hot-standby infrastructure, and associated network and AD replication configuration would materially expand the infrastructure, network, and AD workstreams, almost certainly exceed the 15% budget variance threshold on a $2,400,000 CAD approved budget (triggering Treasury Board notification), and introduce serious schedule risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live and June 30, 2026 closure deadlines with only ~7–8 weeks remaining to Go-Live at the time of the request. The alignment score is set near zero (0.05) rather than exactly 0.0 to reflect that the meeting notes themselves are procedurally compliant — the PM correctly identified the change management obligations and committed to no unilateral scope action — but the substantive ask is almost entirely outside the established baseline."
}

The options analysis must include: (1) a full scope impact assessment against SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001, (2) a cost estimate to determine whether the addition would breach the 15% budget variance threshold triggering mandatory Treasury Board submission, (3) a schedule impact assessment against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint and June 30, 2026 closure deadline — both of which are at significant risk given the March 8, 2026 date of this request, (4) identification of any SA&A implications under ITSG-33 for a new colocation facility, and (5) a recommendation on whether the DR site is better deferred to a subsequent phase (e.g., NGIS Phase 4 or a standalone DR initiative). The addition of a new physical site (Borden or Miramichi), hot-standby infrastructure, and associated network and AD replication configuration would materially expand the infrastructure, network, and AD workstreams, almost certainly exceed the 15% budget variance threshold on a $2,400,000 CAD approved budget (triggering Treasury Board notification), and introduce serious schedule risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live and June 30, 2026 closure deadlines with only ~7–8 weeks remaining to Go-Live at the time of the request.   "reasoning": "All five items reported in this status email map directly and cleanly to in-scope baseline activities. GPO consolidation and migration from Sites B and C is a natural and expected sub-task of the DC migration workstream — no new application code, architecture changes, or out-of-scope platform work is implied. Backup and rollback procedures are standard execution activities within the Migration Runbook (D-004) workstream. The Catalyst 9500 switch dependency is consistent with the in-scope switch deployment deliverable. The March 14 staging target and March 18 migration window are well within the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006), preserving schedule buffer. No budget implications, no new requirements, no contradictions with out-of-scope items, and no change management triggers are present. The 0.03 deduction from a perfect score reflects a minor implicit risk: the switch delivery dependency is flagged but unresolved, and any delay could compress the four-day buffer before the migration window, warranting active monitoring by the PM."
}

```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.05,
  "drift_type": "contradicts_scope",
  "detected_ask": "Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured before April 15, 2026.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'",
  "suggested_action": "Formally reject the scope addition under the SSC Project Change Management Process and issue a written response to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre confirming that end-user device replacement is explicitly out of scope for NGIS Phase 3 and has been assigned to NGIS Phase 4. Do not table this request at the steering committee as a candidate for approval within this project. If the operational need is urgent, redirect the service delivery manager to the NGIS Phase 4 project sponsor or the appropriate procurement authority. Document the request and the rejection decision in the project change log for audit purposes.",
  "reasoning": "The request directly contradicts an explicitly named out-of-scope item in the baseline. The scope baseline unambiguously excludes end-user device refresh or replacement — including laptops and desktops — and assigns that work to NGIS Phase 4. The operational rationale offered (co-location disruption, aging hardware, avoiding a separate project) does not constitute a valid scope justification under the SSC Project Change Management Process; it is a convenience argument that does not alter the formal scope boundary. Additionally, the estimated cost of $210,000–$240,000 CAD would consume approximately 8.75%–10% of the total approved budget of $2,400,000 CAD, a material impact that would require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure. The proposed delivery date of April 15, 2026 also falls only 15 days before the Go-Live milestone (D-006, April 30, 2026), introducing schedule risk to a constrained critical path. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the only marginal connection to the project is the physical relocation context; the substance of the request is entirely outside the approved scope."
}
```

The proposed delivery date of April 15, 2026 also falls only 15 days before the Go-Live milestone (D-006, April 30, 2026), introducing schedule risk to a constrained critical path.
